content: Just as the linguistic meaning of 'reflexion' in high school English curricula inherently encompasses three linked functions—reflecting light (input mirroring), reflecting context (representation), and deliberate reflection (critical re-evaluation), LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be redesigned to explicitly structure their dynamic memory around this tripartite linguistic framework to improve multi-step reasoning. This framing turns a basic vocabulary entry into a actionable design blueprint for autonomous LLM agents.
